facts.statement: Sandisk Corp trades on Nasdaq under ticker SNDK, and the SEC submissions metadata lists its SIC description as "Computer Storage Devices."
Prior to February 21, 2025, Sandisk was wholly owned by Western Digital Corporation; as of February 21, 2025, Sandisk separated from WDC and became a standalone publicly traded company.
Sandisk describes itself as a developer, manufacturer and provider of data storage devices and solutions based on NAND flash technology.
Sandisk's solutions include solid-state drives, embedded products, removable cards, universal serial bus drives, and wafers and components.

Sandisk says its flash storage portfolio serves AI workloads in data centers, edge devices, and consumers, and enables use cases ranging from students, gamers and home offices to enterprises and public clouds.
The 10-K XBRL taxonomy identifies Cloud, Client Devices, and Consumer as Sandisk revenue category members for fiscal 2025, 2024, and 2023 periods.
The SEC company facts summary reports FY2025 revenue of $6.086 billion under RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax.
The SEC company facts summary reports FY2025 net income of negative $2.143 billion.
The SEC company facts summary reports FY2025 operating cash flow of negative $713 million.
The SEC company facts summary reports FY2025 research and development expense of $1.167 billion.
The SEC company facts summary reports FY2025 stockholders' equity of $12.977 billion, cash and equivalents of $328 million, and long-term debt noncurrent of $0.

In the proxy Appendix A reconciliation for the year ended June 27, 2025, Sandisk reported GAAP operating loss of $1.377 billion and non-GAAP operating income of $689 million after adjustments including stock-based compensation, separation costs, employee termination and other, goodwill impairment, and other items.

Sandisk recognized a $1.8 billion goodwill impairment charge for the three months ended March 28, 2025 after identifying potential impairment indicators related to its trading price and market capitalization following the separation.

Sandisk says it periodically incurs charges to realign operations with anticipated market demand; in fiscal 2025 it recorded $21 million of employee termination and other charges, including $18 million of employee termination benefits.

In connection with a strategic decision to outsource manufacturing of certain components and assemblies, Sandisk completed the sale of 80% of its equity interest in one manufacturing subsidiary on September 28, 2024, resulting in a $34 million gain on business divestiture in the proxy non-GAAP reconciliation.

Sandisk's summarized non-consolidated joint venture information includes Flash Ventures, the Unis Venture, and SDSS for fiscal 2025; those investees had combined net sales of $2.315 billion, gross loss of $101 million, and net loss of $63 million in fiscal 2025.
The proxy cautionary note identifies reliance on strategic relationships with key partners, including Kioxia Corporation, as a risk factor for Sandisk.

A substantial portion of Sandisk's manufacturing operations in Malaysia operate under tax holidays and incentive programs expiring in whole or in part during 2028 through 2031; these incentives increased 2025 net earnings by $82 million, or $0.56 per share.

Sandisk's 2025 10-K was signed by David V. Goeckeler as Chief Executive Officer and Director, Luis F. Visoso as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, and Michael R. Pokorny as Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer.
For the 2025 annual meeting, Sandisk nominated seven directors: Richard B. Cassidy II, Thomas Caulfield, David V. Goeckeler, Devinder Kumar, Necip Sayiner, Ellyn J. Shook, and Miyuki Suzuki.
The 2025 proxy states that Sandisk's director nominees are 86% independent, 29% women, and have an average age of 66 years.

Sandisk's proxy letter says Matthew Massengill and Kimberly Alexy, two current Western Digital directors appointed to Sandisk's board for leadership continuity through the separation, will not be nominated for reelection at the 2025 annual meeting.

The proxy Appendix A states that Sandisk's Compensation and Talent Committee used non-GAAP operating income as a pre-established performance goal under the short-term incentive plan for the second half of fiscal 2025.

Sandisk's proxy cautionary note identifies risks including volatility in product demand, pricing trends and average selling price fluctuations, competitive products and pricing, technology transitions, manufacturing or supply chain disruptions, product defects, and business or market conditions.
